blocks.content: The word we landed on the table like something dropped from a height.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I'm not saying anything you're not already thinking. I'm just saying it.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That's not — we've known each other for twenty years. We're not—

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I know that. I'm talking about what Davenport is thinking. Not what I'm thinking.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Which is why we need to be clear. About where everyone was. About everything that happened this weekend.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We talked to Davenport individually. That's different. We haven't talked to each other.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: He told us not to discuss the details of the evening before we each gave our individual accounts. We've done that. We're past that.
Nobody argued the point. Dean looked around the table — at Cade, at Sophie, at Wren, at Nadia.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I think we need to know where everyone was yesterday. All of us. Because if the police are going to look at us — and they are going to look at us — we need to understand what they're going to find.
